Off Calendar Dec-16-2021 Continued To Feb-23-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. STEVEN G. ZIEFF, Esq. and WILLIAM P. MCELHINNY, Esq. of Rudy, Exelrod, Zieff & Lowe, LLP (Tel: 415-434-9800) for plaintiff;
Attys. SETH E. PIERCE, Esq. and COREY G. SINGER, Esq. of Mitchell Silberberg & Knupp LLP (Tel: 310-312-2000) for defendant the Walt Disney Company and Foxnext, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS THE WALT DISNEY COMPANY and FOXNEXT, LLC's Motion for Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is continued to February 23, 2022. Defendants shall file and serve a single "Revised Motion for Summary Judgment or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication" that attacks the First Amended Complaint by January 10, 2022. Per the conditions agreed to in this court's order of September 9, 2021 granting leave to amend the complaint, plaintiff shall file and serve its opposition to the revised motion by February 10, 2022. Defendants' reply shall be filed and served by February 16, 2022. Defendant is advised to reformat its separate statement to include only material and non-repetitive facts. (See Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 283-284, 290.) Defendants' present iteration of its motion/supplemental motion include repetitive separate statements of undisputed facts that are over 1200 pages, which the court concludes is abusive. Defendants' opening brief and plaintiff's opposing brief shall be 20 pages. The reply shall be 10 pages.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-30-2021 Continued To Dec-16-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-09-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
William McElhinny, Esq. (415-434-9800; wpm@rezlaw.com) of Rudy, Exelrod, Zieff & Lowe, LLP for Plaintiff.
Seth Pierce, Esq. (310-312-2000; sep@msk.com) and Corey Singer, Esq. (cgs@msk.com) of Mitchell Silberberg & Knupp LLP for Defendants.

A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF DANIEL ERICKSON'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted on the following conditions: (1) counsel shall promptly make Plaintiff available for further deposition on the three added claims; and (2) if defendants wish to proceed with their pending motion for summary judgment on December 16, 2021, rather than refiling their motion, the court may hear that motion despite the filing of the first amended complaint, and defendants may supplement that motion to include the newly pled claims on 30 days notice, rather than the 75 days otherwise required by statute. The motion is supported by the strong policy in favor of liberal amendment of pleadings. Plaintiff has not unduly delayed seeking leave to amend, and defendants do not show that they would be prejudiced by the amendment in light of the conditions and the current March 21, 2022 trial date. If defendants seek to challenge the three additional claims plaintiff seeks leave to add, they may do so by appropriate motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
